steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text suggests about the Turkins. The key line: residents ‘always pointed to the Turkins as the most accomplished and most enlightened family of all.’ That ‘always’ and superlative phrasing mark them as a standout.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'relative newcomers to the city of S.': The passage says newcomers complain about S., not that the Turkins are newcomers. ✗
  • Choice B — 'unique status in the city of S.': Being singled out as ‘the most accomplished and most enlightened family of all’ gives them a clearly distinctive standing. ✓
  • Choice C — 'amused by other residents': It is the residents who point to the Turkins, not the other way around; no amusement is described. ✗
  • Choice D — 'long disliked living in the city of S.': Dissatisfaction with S. belongs to unnamed newcomers, not to the Turkins. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
